is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum malaria in children; inclusion required 'P. falciparum infection with no other Plasmodium species detected.'

reports_target_markers.evidence: Genotyped at dhfr 51,59,108 and dhps 437,540,581,613 — all curated codons. Prevalences given: dhfr-59 66%, dhps-437 72%, dhps-540 67%.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Figure 2 gives prevalence % per codon (n=212), and text gives dhfr-108/51 99%, dhfr-59 66%, dhps-437 72%, dhps-540 67%, quintuple mutant 43% (92/212).

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples collected in Rutshuru, a district in Eastern DRC (four clinics) — a sub-national location, not just country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Trial conducted between June and September 2002 — a single collection window well within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children 6-59 months collected in Rutshuru, DRC, an African country.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary therapeutic efficacy trial with genotyping of 212 field samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: New field collection from a therapeutic efficacy trial in Rutshuru 2002; genotyping funded by Global Network for Women's and Children's Health Research. No indication these specific samples were published elsewhere (contrasted with other DRC studies).

supplement.evidence: Per-locus frequencies are presented in Figure 2 and text within the main paper; no pointer to supplementary data for extraction.

assessment.summary: Primary SP therapeutic efficacy trial in Rutshuru, Eastern DRC (June-Sept 2002), genotyping 212 P. falciparum field samples at dhfr (51,59,108) and dhps (437,540,581,613) codons. Figure 2 provides per-codon prevalence (n=212), giving extractable frequencies at a sub-national location within a tight temporal window. All criteria appear met.
